Right of Way Relocation Agent - Remote (ME, VT, NH, MA, CT, RI & NY) (on-call)

  • ORC - 4.1
  • Connecticut

  • Quick Apply

Job Details

  • Part-time | Per diem
  • $37.50 - $44.00 an hour

Benefits

  • Travel reimbursement
  • 401(k)
  • Referral program
  • Paid sick time

Qualifications

  • Computer operation
  • Microsoft Outlook
  • iOS
  • Driver's License
  • Dropbox
  • Managing real estate transactions
  • Certified Notary Public
  • Right-of-way
  • Negotiation
  • Mobile devices

Full Job Description

Job Type
On-call
Exemption Type
Non-Exempt
Location
CT US
MA US
ME US
NH US
NY US (Primary)
RI US
VT US
Category
Industry Professional
Travel
50%
Compensation Range
$37.50-$44 per hour
Job Description

Location: Remote (candidate will have the flexibility to work out of their home office but must reside in Maine, Vermont, New Hampshire, Massachusetts, Connecticut, Rhode Island, or New York)
Schedule: Part-time, On-call, Non-exempt
Travel: Candidate should expect to travel about 50% of the time throughout New England states and New York.

Why Join ORC?
The Right Work. The Right People. The Right Culture.
Right of Way is where infrastructure meets innovation—shaping roads, utilities, and communities for the future. At ORC, we offer more than a job—we offer purpose, stability, and growth. You’ll join a team that values collaboration, workplace flexibility, and long-term opportunity.

What We Offer

  • Competitive base pay: $37.50-$44 per hour
  • 401(k)
  • Paid sick time
  • ClassPass and Breethe Memberships

What You'll Do

  • Provide land acquisition and relocation services throughout the right-of-way lifecycle, in compliance with project guidelines and regulations
  • Review title reports and updates, ROW impact and construction plans, project impact valuations and offer presentation documents, among others; for accuracy and reiteration to impacted owners and other project stakeholders
  • Deliver property rights documents to owners and representatives; negotiate and secure parcels through approved counteroffers
  • Maintain contact documentation, conduct final title checks, manage back taxes, and ensure removal of compensated items
  • Submit and track offer letters, counteroffers, and Releases of Damages; attend field reviews and provide weekly status reports
  • Handle administrative tasks including mailing notifications, filing deeds, verifying condemnation parcel addresses, and organizing acquisition packets
  • Submit vendor forms to expedite payments and support efficient project closeout

We are looking for someone who

  • Works efficiently and effectively under tight deadlines
  • Can prioritize and balance multiple tasks
  • Demonstrates strong organization and planning skills
  • Is analytical, detail-oriented, and eager to learn
  • Has excellent written and verbal communication skills
  • Thrives in a fast-paced administrative setting
  • Provides strong customer service experience
  • Can adapt to new systems and software environments

Minimum Requirements

  • Minimum of six (6) year of right of way acquisition experience
    • Must include experience negotiating for property rights and relocating displaced individuals and businesses with minimal oversight
  • Active Notary OR the ability to obtain within one year of employment
  • Must be proficient with MS Office Suite, specifically Excel, Word, Outlook, and Dropbox
  • Must have an iOS or Android smartphone to be able to access ORC's systems
  • Valid Driver’s License

Bonus Points if you have IRWA or equivalent certifications in corresponding ROW disciplines!

Relocation and per diem are not provided

Ready to Apply?
Make your mark on projects that matter. Apply now to join a supportive team that invests in your future.

Our job titles may span more than one career level. Compensation for this position is dependent upon many factors, such as training, transferable skills, work experience, business needs, and market demands. The base pay range is subject to change and may be modified in the future. This role may also be eligible for benefits and employee travel reimbursements.

ORC is one of America’s most respected right-of-way acquisition firms. Our services not only include providing outsourced right-of-way services to public agencies, but also electric transmission, oil and gas pipelines, fiber optic, and sewer and water infrastructure. We are excited to be part of the renewable energy forefront for wind and solar projects.

Employees must avoid any relationship or activity that might impair, or even appear to impair, their ability to make objective and fair decisions when performing their jobs. To avoid conflicts of interest, employees are prohibited from performing any services for clients or perceived clients during nonworking time that are normally performed by ORC, including the sale of real estate.

Physical Requirements: While performing the duties of this job, the employee is regularly required to clearly communicate and exchange information in person, via phone and email. Substantial repetitive movements of the wrists, hands and/or fingers due to high computer usage and typing. Able to read, understand and interpret department related documents and data. The employee is required to move about inside the office to access files, office equipment and to interact with other members of the staff. This role requires the employee to travel to meet with clients or vendors which involves time driving; sometimes for long durations. While meeting with clients and vendors, will need to be outdoors traversing different types of landscapes. May be exposed to the elements (heat, rain, snow, cold).


Employee and Applicant Rights and Responsibilities EEO is the Law All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, disability, or status as a protected veteran. These protections extend to all management policies, practices and decisions, including recruitment and hiring, appraisal systems, promotions, training and career development programs. EEO Policy Statement EOE, including disability/vets In order to be considered for employment, it is ORC's policy for all candidates to complete an online application for a specific job opening. ORC will not accept paper applications or resumes that are received in any other electronic format, unless an accommodation is requested. ORC is an equal opportunity/affirmative action employer. If you need accommodation for any part of the employment process because of a medical condition or disability, please contact Human Resources to let us know the nature of your request.

Benefits:

  • 401(k)
  • Referral program

Application Question(s):

  • Do you have experience negotiating for property rights and relocating displaced individuals and businesses with minimal oversight?
  • Do you have IRWA or equivalent certifications in corresponding ROW disciplines?

Experience:

  • Right of Way Acquisition: 6 years (Required)

License/Certification:

  • Certified Notary Public (Preferred)

Work Location: In person
